title: Expose a dev checkbox for the direct CUA disableNavigation capture bit
summary: 0.6.61 keeps the current thumbstick-click grab-navigation override seam and adds a narrow development checkbox that gates only the direct SuperController.disableNavigation capture bit while the player owns CUA focus.
changes: The current CUA grab-navigation override remains thumbstick-click only; the later Unity joystick button 5 fallback stays out.
The player plugin UI now exposes a FrameAngel Player Disable Navigation checkbox in the CUA input section.
That checkbox gates only the direct disableNavigation bit while capture is active; the rest of the focus and input-capture seam stays unchanged.
reasoning: The live CUA input seam already has a single direct navigation-disable bit in the snapshot path, but it was not operator-controllable. Exposing only that bit as a temporary development control is the narrowest safe way to avoid getting caught by navigation disable behavior without reopening the broader grab-nav and focus model.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: The old Unity button 5 fallback remains backed out; live override behavior still comes from thumbstick-click only.
The plugin now shows a FrameAngel Player Disable Navigation checkbox for development use.
Turning that checkbox off should stop the player from forcing SuperController.disableNavigation while capture is active, without changing the rest of the CUA focus path.
knownIssues: This slice does not change movie transport, image transport, shuffle policy, slideshow, right-stick volume, or deferred gaze work.
This slice does not claim to fully solve the remaining grab-nav paradigm.
verification: ReadCuaPlayerGrabNavigateOverrideActive still only checks OVR thumbstick buttons in FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs.
The new FrameAngel Player Disable Navigation JSONStorableBool is built, registered, and added to the CUA input UI in FASyncRuntime.CuaPlayerInputModule.cs.
SetCuaPlayerInputCaptureState now gates only desired.disableNavigation through that checkbox.
